---
title: "Dissimilarity Analysis - Hogup Cave & Crescent Springs"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
    theme: cosmo
---

Establish occupation periods defined by Martin et al. 2017
```{r setup, include=FALSE}
############################################################
# WORKFLOW FOR HOGUP–WETLAND DISSIMILARITY ANALYSIS
# Using Martin et al. (2017) phases, ordered Monte Carlo ages,
# and two analyses for the Stratum 9 residual sample.
############################################################
if (!require("here")) install.packages("here")
library(here)
library(analogue)
library(magrittr)
```

```{r occphases}
## STEP 1.1: Define Martin et al. (2017) Phase boundaries ----
## These are the phase windows you will use for pollen ages. [file:2]

phase_bounds <- list(
  I  = c(start = 9790, end = 6490),  # Strata 1–7
  II = c(start = 5840, end = 3330),  # Stratum 8
  III= c(start = 2870, end = 2760),  # Strata 9–10
  IV = c(start = 2610, end =  360)   # Strata 11–16
)
```

```{r maptooccphases}
## STEP 1.2: Map each cave pollen sample (row) to a Phase ----

hogup_meta <- read.csv(here("..", "data", "raw", "hogup_pollen.csv"), check.names = TRUE)

# Function to map stratum to phase label
stratum_to_phase <- function(stratum) {
  if (stratum >= 1  & stratum <= 7)  return("I")
  if (stratum == 8)                  return("II")
  if (stratum == 9 | stratum == 10)  return("III")
  if (stratum >= 11 & stratum <= 16) return("IV")
  return(NA_character_)
}

hogup_meta$phase <- vapply(hogup_meta$stratum, stratum_to_phase, character(1))
hogup_meta$phase <- factor(hogup_meta$phase, levels = c("I", "II", "III", "IV"))
```

```{r confirmmapping}
## STEP 1.3: Confirm mapping from phases to age windows ----

table(hogup_meta$stratum, hogup_meta$phase)
```

```{r timestep}
## STEP 1.4: Choose a global time step for interpolation ----

time_step <- 250   # time step in years; adjust if needed
```

Ordered Monte Carlo age assignments for Hogup Cave samples
```{r ordersamples}
## STEP 2.1: Order samples within each phase by stratigraphy ----
## We assume lower stratum number = older.

# Separate pollen data from metadata
meta_cols   <- c("sample", "stratum", "phase")
cave_pollen <- hogup_meta[, !colnames(hogup_meta) %in% meta_cols, drop = FALSE]
hogup_meta  <- hogup_meta[,  meta_cols]

# Set row names to sample ID to enforce alignment
rownames(cave_pollen) <- hogup_meta$sample
rownames(hogup_meta)  <- hogup_meta$sample

# Sort by stratum within phase (lower stratum number = older)
hogup_meta  <- hogup_meta[order(hogup_meta$phase, hogup_meta$stratum), ]
cave_pollen <- cave_pollen[rownames(hogup_meta), ]

```

```{r countsamples}
## STEP 2.2: Count samples per phase ----

samples_per_phase <- table(hogup_meta$phase)
samples_per_phase
```

```{r generateages}
## STEP 2.3: Function to generate one set of ordered ages for all samples ----

generate_ordered_ages <- function(meta, phase_bounds) {
  # meta: hogup_meta with columns phase, phase_start, phase_end
  # Returns: numeric vector of ages aligned with rows of 'meta'
  
  ages <- numeric(nrow(meta))   # placeholder
  
  # Loop over phases
  for (ph in names(phase_bounds)) {
    idx <- which(meta$phase == ph)           # indices in this phase
    if (length(idx) == 0) next               # skip if no samples
    n   <- length(idx)                       # number of samples in phase
    
    # Draw and sort proportions across the whole phase
    p   <- sort(runif(n, 0, 1))              # ordered U(0,1)

    # Map to ages using phase start and end
    start <- phase_bounds[[ph]]["start"]
    end   <- phase_bounds[[ph]]["end"]
    ages_phase <- start + p * (end - start)  # ordered ages within phase

    # Shuffle ages within each stratum so within-stratum order is random,
    # while between-stratum ordering (older stratum = older ages) is preserved
    strata_in_phase <- meta$stratum[idx]
    for (s in unique(strata_in_phase)) {
      s_pos <- which(strata_in_phase == s)
      ages_phase[s_pos] <- sample(ages_phase[s_pos], size = length(s_pos))
    }

    # Assign to these rows (meta is sorted by stratum within phase)
    ages[idx] <- ages_phase
  }
  
  return(ages)
}
```

```{r residualsamples}
## STEP 2.4: Handle the Stratum 9 residual pollen sample ----

hogup_meta$is_residual <- FALSE
hogup_meta$is_residual[hogup_meta$sample == 357] <- TRUE

residual_age_range <- c(4478, 4873)  # calibrated mid-Holocene range (cal BP)

# Variant 1: generate ages excluding the residual sample (Analysis A)
generate_ordered_ages_excl_resid <- function(meta, phase_bounds) {
  meta_no_resid <- meta[!meta$is_residual, ]
  ages_no_resid <- generate_ordered_ages(meta_no_resid, phase_bounds)
  ages <- rep(NA_real_, nrow(meta))
  ages[!meta$is_residual] <- ages_no_resid
  # residual sample intentionally has NA (excluded)
  return(ages)
}

# Variant 2: generate ages including residual as mid-Holocene (Analysis B)
generate_ordered_ages_incl_resid <- function(meta, phase_bounds, resid_range) {
  meta_no_resid <- meta[!meta$is_residual, ]
  ages_no_resid <- generate_ordered_ages(meta_no_resid, phase_bounds)
  ages <- rep(NA_real_, nrow(meta))
  ages[!meta$is_residual] <- ages_no_resid
  
  # assign residual sample a mid-Holocene age (either fixed or uniform)
  resid_idx <- which(meta$is_residual)
  if (length(resid_idx) == 1) {
    ages[resid_idx] <- runif(1, resid_range[1], resid_range[2])
  }
  
  return(ages)
}
```

```{r montecarlo}
## STEP 2.5: Generate many Monte Carlo realizations of ages ----

n_real <- 200   # number of Monte Carlo realizations (adjust as needed)

# Storage lists
age_realizations_excl <- vector("list", n_real)
age_realizations_incl <- vector("list", n_real)

set.seed(123)   # reproducibility

for (k in 1:n_real) {
  age_realizations_excl[[k]] <- generate_ordered_ages_excl_resid(hogup_meta, phase_bounds)
  age_realizations_incl[[k]] <- generate_ordered_ages_incl_resid(hogup_meta, phase_bounds,
                                                                 resid_range = residual_age_range)
}
```

```{r agenote}
## STEP 2.6: Age vectors now align with rows of cave_pollen ----
## For realization k, cave ages (excluding residual) = age_realizations_excl[[k]]
## For realization k, cave ages (including residual) = age_realizations_incl[[k]]
```

Dissimilarity Analysis w Crescent Springs Core
```{r loadwetdata}
## STEP 3.0: Load Crescent Springs wetland pollen data ----

crescent_raw <- read.csv(here("..", "data", "raw", "crescent_pollen.csv"), check.names = TRUE)

wet_age <- crescent_raw$Age  # cal yr BP

meta_cols  <- c("Depth", "Age", "Pollen.Sum", "Terrestrial", "Lyco")
wet_pollen <- crescent_raw[, !colnames(crescent_raw) %in% meta_cols, drop = FALSE]
rownames(wet_pollen) <- crescent_raw$Depth
```

```{r prepcsdata}
## STEP 3.1: Prepare wetland core and harmonize taxa ----

# Convert to proportions
wet_prop  <- as.data.frame(wet_pollen / 100)
cave_prop <- as.data.frame(cave_pollen / 100)

# Harmonize taxa
common_taxa <- intersect(colnames(wet_prop), colnames(cave_prop))
wet_prop    <- wet_prop[,  common_taxa]
cave_prop   <- cave_prop[, common_taxa]
```

```{r definetimegrid}
## STEP 3.2: Define a common time grid ----

# Use wetland age range and full Hogup phase ranges for bounding
age_min <- max(min(wet_age), min(unlist(lapply(phase_bounds, `[[`, "end"))))
age_max <- min(max(wet_age), max(unlist(lapply(phase_bounds, `[[`, "start"))))

time_grid <- seq(age_min, age_max, by = time_step)
```

```{r intpollenmatrix}
## STEP 3.3: Helper to interpolate a pollen matrix to time_grid ----

interp_pollen <- function(age_vec, prop_mat, new_ages) {
  # age_vec: vector of sample ages (cal BP)
  # prop_mat: rows = samples, cols = taxa (proportions)
  # new_ages: desired ages (time_grid)
  
  ord <- order(age_vec)
  age_vec  <- age_vec[ord]
  prop_mat <- prop_mat[ord, , drop = FALSE]
  
  interp_mat <- sapply(
    1:ncol(prop_mat),
    function(j) approx(x = age_vec,
                       y = prop_mat[, j],
                       xout = new_ages,
                       rule = 2)$y
  )
  interp_df <- as.data.frame(interp_mat)
  colnames(interp_df) <- colnames(prop_mat)
  rownames(interp_df) <- as.character(new_ages)
  
  # renormalize rows to sum to 1
  rs <- rowSums(interp_df)
  interp_df <- sweep(interp_df, 1, rs, "/")
  
  return(interp_df)
}
```

Squared Chord Distance & RoC Functions
```{r dissfunctions}
## STEP 3.4: Functions for squared chord distance and RoC ----

compute_roc <- function(prop_df, ages) {
  # prop_df rows must correspond to 'ages' (time_grid)
  scd <- analogue::distance(
    x = prop_df[-nrow(prop_df), , drop = FALSE],
    y = prop_df[-1,           , drop = FALSE],
    method = "SQchord"
  )
  age_mid <- (ages[-nrow(prop_df)] + ages[-1]) / 2
  data.frame(age_mid = age_mid, SCD = diag(scd))
}

compute_between <- function(wet_df, cave_df, ages) {
  # wet_df, cave_df on same time_grid
  scd <- analogue::distance(
    x = wet_df,
    y = cave_df,
    method = "SQchord"
  )
  data.frame(age = ages, SCD = diag(scd))
}

# Run Monte Carlo dissimilarity analyses
# Storage: lists of list (one list per realization)
roc_wet_A  <- vector("list", n_real)
roc_cave_A <- vector("list", n_real)
between_A  <- vector("list", n_real)

roc_wet_B  <- vector("list", n_real)
roc_cave_B <- vector("list", n_real)
between_B  <- vector("list", n_real)

# Wetland interpolation is identical across all realizations
wet_interp <- interp_pollen(wet_age, wet_prop, time_grid)

for (k in 1:n_real) {
  # Analysis A: exclude residual
  cave_age_A   <- age_realizations_excl[[k]]
  cave_interp_A <- interp_pollen(cave_age_A[!hogup_meta$is_residual],
                                 cave_prop[!hogup_meta$is_residual, ],
                                 time_grid)
  
  roc_wet_A[[k]]  <- compute_roc(wet_interp,  time_grid)
  roc_cave_A[[k]] <- compute_roc(cave_interp_A, time_grid)
  between_A[[k]]  <- compute_between(wet_interp, cave_interp_A, time_grid)
  
  # Analysis B: include residual as mid-Holocene
  cave_age_B   <- age_realizations_incl[[k]]
  cave_interp_B <- interp_pollen(cave_age_B, cave_prop, time_grid)
  
  roc_wet_B[[k]]  <- compute_roc(wet_interp,  time_grid)
  roc_cave_B[[k]] <- compute_roc(cave_interp_B, time_grid)
  between_B[[k]]  <- compute_between(wet_interp, cave_interp_B, time_grid)
}
```

Summarize
```{r summarize}
## STEP 3.5: Summarize across realizations ----

# Helper: stack one SCD column from all realizations into a matrix
stack_metric <- function(list_of_df, colname = "SCD") {
  do.call(cbind, lapply(list_of_df, `[[`, colname))
}

# Helper: compute median and 95% CI envelope from a stacked matrix
summarise_metric <- function(mat, ages, age_col = "age_mid") {
  data.frame(
    setNames(list(ages), age_col),
    median = apply(mat, 1, median,   na.rm = TRUE),
    q025   = apply(mat, 1, quantile, probs = 0.025, na.rm = TRUE),
    q975   = apply(mat, 1, quantile, probs = 0.975, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
}

age_mid_vec <- roc_cave_A[[1]]$age_mid  # shared midpoint ages (RoC metrics)
age_vec     <- between_A[[1]]$age       # shared time-grid ages (between metrics)

# Analysis A
roc_cave_A_summary <- summarise_metric(stack_metric(roc_cave_A), age_mid_vec, "age_mid")
roc_wet_A_summary  <- summarise_metric(stack_metric(roc_wet_A),  age_mid_vec, "age_mid")
between_A_summary  <- summarise_metric(stack_metric(between_A),  age_vec,     "age")

# Analysis B
roc_cave_B_summary <- summarise_metric(stack_metric(roc_cave_B), age_mid_vec, "age_mid")
roc_wet_B_summary  <- summarise_metric(stack_metric(roc_wet_B),  age_mid_vec, "age_mid")
between_B_summary  <- summarise_metric(stack_metric(between_B),  age_vec,     "age")
```
